▪️ Building his own media brand: ‘I’ve never wanted to be pigeon-holed’
▪️ England vs Ghana: ‘Nobody remembers the group stages’

Rio Ferdinand hosted the World Cup draw and is now presenting a show in the U.S. with James Corden.

He talks to @lauriewhitwell about this tournament and why he has chosen to build his own media channels after a decade in broadcasting.

2070098251399786533.headline: Ismael Kone
2070098251399786533.body: “The foul that break Ismael Kone’s leg, committed by Qatari midfielder Assim Madibo, was a reasonably bad one - but not horrendous. “However, it was announced on Wednesday by FIFA that he is to be banned for five games, up from the initial mandatory one game. “It smacks of FIFA trying to make an example of a relatively low-profile player that they knew wouldn’t be a factor in the remainder of the tournament. “Similar fouls have happened without the consequences being so dire, for both the tackler and the tackled. “Hopefully Qatar will appeal and the decision will be reversed. But as things stand, FIFA have reacted to the outcome of an incident, rather than the incident itself.” ✍️ NickMiller79 Free to read: bit.ly/4xPmNTj
2070098251399786533.raw_text:
“The foul that break Ismael Kone’s leg, committed by Qatari midfielder Assim Madibo, was a reasonably bad one - but not horrendous.

“However, it was announced on Wednesday by FIFA that he is to be banned for five games, up from the initial mandatory one game.

“It smacks of FIFA trying to make an example of a relatively low-profile player that they knew wouldn’t be a factor in the remainder of the tournament.

“Similar fouls have happened without the consequences being so dire, for both the tackler and the tackled.

“Hopefully Qatar will appeal and the decision will be reversed. But as things stand, FIFA have reacted to the outcome of an incident, rather than the incident itself.”

✍️ @NickMiller79 

Free to read: bit.ly/4xPmNTj

2069961817598669307.headline: Ismael Kone
2069961817598669307.body: While the majority of the available sympathy should be directed towards Canada's Ismael Kone, who had surgery on the tibia and fibula break and will miss the remainder of the World Cup, plus probably a decent portion of the next domestic season, it’s also impossible not to feel pretty sorry for Assim Madibo too. The decision to give the Qatar midfielder a five-game ban feels like a remarkable overreaction by FIFA. You can only conclude that the severity of the punishment is largely based on the extent of the injury, not the severity of or intent behind the challenge. 📝 NickMiller79 Free to read: bit.ly/4xPmNTj
2069961817598669307.raw_text:
While the majority of the available sympathy should be directed towards Canada's Ismael Kone, who had surgery on the tibia and fibula break and will miss the remainder of the World Cup, plus probably a decent portion of the next domestic season, it’s also impossible not to feel pretty sorry for Assim Madibo too.

The decision to give the Qatar midfielder a five-game ban feels like a remarkable overreaction by FIFA.

You can only conclude that the severity of the punishment is largely based on the extent of the injury, not the severity of or intent behind the challenge.

📝 @NickMiller79

2069978214290583909.headline: Pride Match
2069978214290583909.body: The Iran Football Federation wants FIFA to prevent any “ceremonies or promotional activities” in support of the LGBTQ+ community at a much discussed “Pride Match” between Egypt and Iran on Friday in Seattle, as well as restricting symbols or representations of the Pride movement at Lumen Field. Since Seattle was confirmed to have a June 26 World Cup fixture, the city’s host committee has been preparing activations to mark the occasion, with the match at Lumen Field fixed as Egypt vs. Iran following the draw last December. FIFA told The Athletic on Wednesday it is permitting rainbow flags at all of its World Cup matches this summer. More from AdamCrafton_ 🔗 nyti.ms/3QJ7gUh
2069978214290583909.raw_text:
The Iran Football Federation wants FIFA to prevent any “ceremonies or promotional activities” in support of the LGBTQ+ community at a much discussed “Pride Match” between Egypt and Iran on Friday in Seattle, as well as restricting symbols or representations of the Pride movement at Lumen Field.

Since Seattle was confirmed to have a June 26 World Cup fixture, the city’s host committee has been preparing activations to mark the occasion, with the match at Lumen Field fixed as Egypt vs. Iran following the draw last December.

FIFA told The Athletic on Wednesday it is permitting rainbow flags at all of its World Cup matches this summer.

More from @AdamCrafton_ 

🔗 nyti.ms/3QJ7gUh

2071285550481363376.headline: Manuel Ugarte
2071285550481363376.body: Desperate news. Hope he makes full recovery. Manuel Ugarte (manuugarte8) Ya pasaron unos días después de todo. Tener la lesión más dura que un futbolista puede tener en uno de los partidos más importantes de mi país y haber terminado de esta manera sin poder acompañar a mis compañeros hasta el final en la cancha y sin dejar al país representado como se merece es algo que me va a quedar marcado para mi vida. Tocar fondo me va hacer más fuerte en todo sentido, y siento que este es el caso. Sin duda cuando la vida quiere que pares te lo hace saber de algún modo. Está en mí sacar lo positivo de esto. Triunfar en la vida es volver a empezar cada vez que uno cae. Agradecido Uruguay, ManUtd_Es por estar a disposición desde el minuto que pasó. También muchas gracias a mi familia, amigos y todos los mensajes de apoyo que he recibido en este tiempo. Voy a volver más fuerte. A few days have passed since everything happened. Suffering the most serious injury a footballer can face in one of the most important matches in my country’s history, and seeing it end this way without being able to stay on the pitch and support my teammates until the final whistle, is something that will stay with me for the rest of my life. Hitting rock bottom will make me stronger in every sense, and I truly believe that’s the case here. Without a doubt, when life wants you to stop, it finds a way to let you know. It’s up to me to take the positives from this. Success in life is about starting again every time you fall. I’m incredibly grateful to Uruguay and ManUtd for being there for me from the moment it happened. A huge thank you as well to my family, my friends, and everyone who has sent me messages of support during this time. I’ll be back stronger. —
2071285550481363376.raw_text:
Desperate news. Hope he makes full recovery.


Manuel Ugarte (@manuugarte8)

Ya pasaron unos días después de todo.

Tener la lesión más dura que un futbolista puede tener en uno de los partidos más importantes de mi país y haber terminado de esta manera sin poder acompañar a mis compañeros hasta el final en la cancha y sin dejar al país representado como se merece es algo que me va a quedar marcado para mi vida.

Tocar fondo me va hacer más fuerte en todo sentido, y siento que este es el caso.

Sin duda cuando la vida quiere que pares te lo hace saber de algún modo.

Está en mí sacar lo positivo de esto.

Triunfar en la vida es volver a empezar cada vez que uno cae.

Agradecido @Uruguay, @ManUtd_Es por estar a disposición desde el minuto que pasó.

También muchas gracias a mi familia, amigos y todos los mensajes de apoyo que he recibido en este tiempo.

Voy a volver más fuerte. 

A few days have passed since everything happened.

Suffering the most serious injury a footballer can face in one of the most important matches in my country’s history, and seeing it end this way without being able to stay on the pitch and support my teammates until the final whistle, is something that will stay with me for the rest of my life.
Hitting rock bottom will make me stronger in every sense, and I truly believe that’s the case here.
Without a doubt, when life wants you to stop, it finds a way to let you know. It’s up to me to take the positives from this.
Success in life is about starting again every time you fall.
I’m incredibly grateful to @Uruguay and @ManUtd for being there for me from the moment it happened. A huge thank you as well to my family, my friends, and everyone who has sent me messages of support during this time.
I’ll be back stronger.

2071526965362758124.headline: World Cup
2071526965362758124.body: How far can Canada go in this World Cup? On the evidence of this, not massively deep into the tournament, although momentum may well snowball from this stage given all three host countries appear to have been able to raise their levels for this ‘home’ tournament. Up next for Canada is a trip to Houston, Texas, on Saturday, which just so happens to be Independence Day in the United States, with either the Netherlands or Morocco, who face off in Monterrey, Mexico, on Monday, lying in wait in the round of 16. Whoever they take on at the Houston Stadium next weekend likely to head into the match as significant favourites. Beyond that, France or Germany would be expected to reach (and be massive favourites for) the quarter-final, which takes place in Boston on July 9, before Spain, Portugal or Belgium probably lie in wait in the last four. Canada fans will rightly dream about how far they can advance, as that is what being a supporter is all about, but a lot of football has to be played before those showpiece matches. ✍️ ChrisDHWaugh 🔗 nyti.ms/4oOUuAc
2071526965362758124.raw_text:
How far can Canada go in this World Cup?

On the evidence of this, not massively deep into the tournament, although momentum may well snowball from this stage given all three host countries appear to have been able to raise their levels for this ‘home’ tournament.

Up next for Canada is a trip to Houston, Texas, on Saturday, which just so happens to be Independence Day in the United States, with either the Netherlands or Morocco, who face off in Monterrey, Mexico, on Monday, lying in wait in the round of 16.

Whoever they take on at the Houston Stadium next weekend likely to head into the match as significant favourites.

Beyond that, France or Germany would be expected to reach (and be massive favourites for) the quarter-final, which takes place in Boston on July 9, before Spain, Portugal or Belgium probably lie in wait in the last four.

Canada fans will rightly dream about how far they can advance, as that is what being a supporter is all about, but a lot of football has to be played before those showpiece matches.

✍️ @ChrisDHWaugh 

🔗 nyti.ms/4oOUuAc

2071585382148243464.raw_text:
🚨🔵 OFFICIAL: Enzo Maresca statement on Chelsea exit.

“The decision to leave was only mine. My resignation from Chelsea opened a path for me to join Manchester City, which is a club I knew very well. I am ecstatic that I have now joined Manchester City”.

“I recognise that my departure from Chelsea in the middle of the season caused disruption for the club and I apologise for that. It was neither my intention nor my wish”.

2071584382133969345.headline: Enzo Maresca
2071584382133969345.body: 🚨⚠️ OFFICIAL: Chelsea statement on Enzo Maresca. “Chelsea FC appreciates the 2025/26 season was a hugely disappointing one for the Club and its supporters. A major contributing factor was the disruption caused as a result of changes that the Club was forced to make to the Head Coach position over the Christmas period”. “Due to recent developments, we consider that it is important to explain to our supporters what happened and why our former Head Coach left the Club on 1 January 2026”. “In Autumn last year, the Club was informed by our former Head Coach that there might be an opportunity for him to succeed Pep Guardiola at the end of the season”. “It became clear to us that it was his strong desire to succeed Guardiola and that he was fully committed to pursuing the opportunity, despite the fact he was under a long-term contract which he had no right to terminate”. “In December 2025, our Head Coach unexpectedly and abruptly resigned from his position. Obviously, we felt let down as we believed that his head and heart were focused on another club and another opportunity, despite having just arrived at Chelsea the year before”. “No club wants to change its head coach midway through a season. However, in light of his decision not to continue fulfilling his responsibilities through to the end of the season, the Club was left with no choice but to protect our players, our supporters, and the Badge and accept his resignation”. “In the circumstances and given the mutual respect between clubs, a confidential settlement has been reached with Manchester City, which includes the payment of compensation. A confidential settlement has also been reached with the former Head Coach under which he will pay compensation”.
2071584382133969345.raw_text:
🚨⚠️ OFFICIAL: Chelsea statement on Enzo Maresca.

“Chelsea FC appreciates the 2025/26 season was a hugely disappointing one for the Club and its supporters. A major contributing factor was the disruption caused as a result of changes that the Club was forced to make to the Head Coach position over the Christmas period”.

“Due to recent developments, we consider that it is important to explain to our supporters what happened and why our former Head Coach left the Club on 1 January 2026”.

“In Autumn last year, the Club was informed by our former Head Coach that there might be an opportunity for him to succeed Pep Guardiola at the end of the season”.

“It became clear to us that it was his strong desire to succeed Guardiola and that he was fully committed to pursuing the opportunity, despite the fact he was under a long-term contract which he had no right to terminate”. 

“In December 2025, our Head Coach unexpectedly and abruptly resigned from his position. Obviously, we felt let down as we believed that his head and heart were focused on another club and another opportunity, despite having just arrived at Chelsea the year before”.

“No club wants to change its head coach midway through a season. However, in light of his decision not to continue fulfilling his responsibilities through to the end of the season, the Club was left with no choice but to protect our players, our supporters, and the Badge and accept his resignation”.

“In the circumstances and given the mutual respect between clubs, a confidential settlement has been reached with Manchester City, which includes the payment of compensation. A confidential settlement has also been reached with the former Head Coach under which he will pay compensation”.

2071580326544220251.raw_text:
🚨 Manchester City confirm Enzo Maresca as manager on a three-year contract.

🗣️ Maresca: "Manchester City is a club I know very well and to have the chance to manage this team is a brilliant opportunity for me.

"City is an incredibly well-run football club. Everything they do is innovative, planned and purposeful. For a manager, that is a dream situation."

2071584720698396834.headline: Enzo Maresca
2071584720698396834.body: 🚨 Chelsea have received £17m in compensation following Enzo Maresca's Manchester City appointment. And Maresca has already agreed a separate settlement Maresca has publicly apologised following his Chelsea's resignation, while Chelsea say they feel let down by the Italian's departure since his head and heart were focused on another club. 🗣️ Maresca: The decision was only mine. My resignation from Chelsea opened a path for me to join Manchester City, which is a club I knew very well. I am ecstatic that I have now joined Manchester City. I recognise that my departure from Chelsea in the middle of the season caused disruption for the club and I apologise for that. It was neither my intention nor my wish. Maresca was given permission to speak to Manchester City last year, but at the time Chelsea felt it would be with a view to continuing until the end of the season at the earliest. 🗣️ Chelsea statement: Chelsea FC appreciates the 2025/26 season was a hugely disappointing one for the Club and its supporters. A major contributing factor was the disruption caused as a result of changes that the Club was forced to make to the Head Coach position over the Christmas period. Due to recent developments, we consider that it is important to explain to our supporters what happened and why our former Head Coach left the Club on 1 January 2026. In Autumn last year, the Club was informed by our former Head Coach that there might be an opportunity for him to succeed Pep Guardiola at the end of the season. It became clear to us that it was his strong desire to succeed Guardiola and that he was fully committed to pursuing the opportunity, despite the fact he was under a long-term contract which he had no right to terminate. In December 2025, our Head Coach unexpectedly and abruptly resigned from his position. Obviously, we felt let down as we believed that his head and heart were focused on another club and another opportunity, despite having just arrived at Chelsea the year before. No club wants to change its head coach midway through a season. However, in light of his decision not to continue fulfilling his responsibilities through to the end of the season, the Club was left with no choice but to protect our players, our supporters, and the Badge and accept his resignation. In the circumstances and given the mutual respect between clubs, a confidential settlement has been reached with Manchester City, which includes the payment of compensation. A confidential settlement has also been reached with the former Head Coach under which he will pay compensation. Looking forward to next season, in Xabi Alonso, we have a Manager who has an exceptional football mind and is a professional of the highest integrity. He has all the attributes to deliver the success the Club’s supporters deserve and expect.
2071584720698396834.raw_text:
🚨 Chelsea have received £17m in compensation following Enzo Maresca's Manchester City appointment. And Maresca has already agreed a separate settlement

Maresca has publicly apologised following his Chelsea's resignation, while Chelsea say they feel "let down" by the Italian's departure since his "head and heart were focused on another club".

🗣️ Maresca: "The decision was only mine. My resignation from Chelsea opened a path for me to join Manchester City, which is a club I knew very well. I am ecstatic that I have now joined Manchester City.   

"I recognise that my departure from Chelsea in the middle of the season caused disruption for the club and I apologise for that. It was neither my intention nor my wish."

Maresca was given permission to speak to Manchester City last year, but at the time Chelsea felt it would be with a view to continuing until the end of the season at the earliest.

🗣️ Chelsea statement: "Chelsea FC appreciates the 2025/26 season was a hugely disappointing one for the Club and its supporters. A major contributing factor was the disruption caused as a result of changes that the Club was forced to make to the Head Coach position over the Christmas period. Due to recent developments, we consider that it is important to explain to our supporters what happened and why our former Head Coach left the Club on 1 January 2026.

"In Autumn last year, the Club was informed by our former Head Coach that there might be an opportunity for him to succeed Pep Guardiola at the end of the season. It became clear to us that it was his strong desire to succeed Guardiola and that he was fully committed to pursuing the opportunity, despite the fact he was under a long-term contract which he had no right to terminate.

"In December 2025, our Head Coach unexpectedly and abruptly resigned from his position. Obviously, we felt let down as we believed that his head and heart were focused on another club and another opportunity, despite having just arrived at Chelsea the year before.

"No club wants to change its head coach midway through a season. However, in light of his decision not to continue fulfilling his responsibilities through to the end of the season, the Club was left with no choice but to protect our players, our supporters, and the Badge and accept his resignation.

"In the circumstances and given the mutual respect between clubs, a confidential settlement has been reached with Manchester City, which includes the payment of compensation. A confidential settlement has also been reached with the former Head Coach under which he will pay compensation.

"Looking forward to next season, in Xabi Alonso, we have a Manager who has an exceptional football mind and is a professional of the highest integrity. He has all the attributes to deliver the success the Club’s supporters deserve and expect."

2071604337433096271.headline: Enzo
2071604337433096271.body: 🗣️ Enzo Maresca on replacing Pep Guardiola. It's a huge challenge. All the success over the last 10-15 years. But it's a nice challenge, exciting. The target is to continue or carry on the last decade... The idea is to continue as much as we can to dominate games and play in the opposition's side. We are going to try to do the most important thing in football, which is to win.
2071604337433096271.raw_text:
🗣️ Enzo Maresca on replacing Pep Guardiola. "It's a huge challenge. All the success over the last 10-15 years. But it's a nice challenge, exciting. The target is to continue or carry on the last decade...

"The idea is to continue as much as we can to dominate games and play in the opposition's side. 

"We are going to try to do the most important thing in football, which is to win."

2071580179156111767.headline: Granit Xhaka
2071580179156111767.body: 🚨⤵️ As revealed yesterday: the deal remains complicated, but still on. New talks between the clubs are taking place today. Granit Xhaka wants the move. Outcome still open. CFC Florian Plettenberg (Plettigoal) 🚨🛜 New talks between Chelsea and Sunderland over Granit Xhaka are scheduled for Monday. Senior executives from both clubs remain in direct contact. ⚠️ The deal remains difficult, but is not off at this stage. Xhaka’s position is clear: he wants to join Chelsea and work under Xabi Alonso. Full agreement on personal terms is already in place, as revealed. 2071583078703857859.headline: Enzo Maresca
2071583078703857859.body: Chelsea FC Statement on Enzo Maresca: 'Chelsea FC appreciates the 2025/26 season was a hugely disappointing one for the Club and its supporters. A major contributing factor was the disruption caused as a result of changes that the Club was forced to make to the Head Coach position over the Christmas period. Due to recent developments, we consider that it is important to explain to our supporters what happened and why our former Head Coach left the Club on 1 January 2026. 'In Autumn last year, the Club was informed by our former Head Coach that there might be an opportunity for him to succeed Pep Guardiola at the end of the season. It became clear to us that it was his strong desire to succeed Guardiola and that he was fully committed to pursuing the opportunity, despite the fact he was under a long-term contract which he had no right to terminate. 'In December 2025, our Head Coach unexpectedly and abruptly resigned from his position. Obviously, we felt let down as we believed that his head and heart were focused on another club and another opportunity, despite having just arrived at Chelsea the year before. 'No club wants to change its head coach midway through a season. However, in light of his decision not to continue fulfilling his responsibilities through to the end of the season, the Club was left with no choice but to protect our players, our supporters, and the Badge and accept his resignation. 'In the circumstances and given the mutual respect between clubs, a confidential settlement has been reached with Manchester City, which includes the payment of compensation. A confidential settlement has also been reached with the former Head Coach under which he will pay compensation. 'Looking forward to next season, in Xabi Alonso, we have a Manager who has an exceptional football mind and is a professional of the highest integrity. He has all the attributes to deliver the success the Club’s supporters deserve and expect.' Chelsea FC (ChelseaFC) Club statement. Link 🔗 chelseafc.com —
2071583078703857859.raw_text:
Chelsea FC Statement on Enzo Maresca:

'Chelsea FC appreciates the 2025/26 season was a hugely disappointing one for the Club and its supporters. A major contributing factor was the disruption caused as a result of changes that the Club was forced to make to the Head Coach position over the Christmas period. Due to recent developments, we consider that it is important to explain to our supporters what happened and why our former Head Coach left the Club on 1 January 2026.

'In Autumn last year, the Club was informed by our former Head Coach that there might be an opportunity for him to succeed Pep Guardiola at the end of the season. It became clear to us that it was his strong desire to succeed Guardiola and that he was fully committed to pursuing the opportunity, despite the fact he was under a long-term contract which he had no right to terminate.

'In December 2025, our Head Coach unexpectedly and abruptly resigned from his position. Obviously, we felt let down as we believed that his head and heart were focused on another club and another opportunity, despite having just arrived at Chelsea the year before.

'No club wants to change its head coach midway through a season. However, in light of his decision not to continue fulfilling his responsibilities through to the end of the season, the Club was left with no choice but to protect our players, our supporters, and the Badge and accept his resignation.

'In the circumstances and given the mutual respect between clubs, a confidential settlement has been reached with Manchester City, which includes the payment of compensation. A confidential settlement has also been reached with the former Head Coach under which he will pay compensation.

'Looking forward to next season, in Xabi Alonso, we have a Manager who has an exceptional football mind and is a professional of the highest integrity. He has all the attributes to deliver the success the Club’s supporters deserve and expect.'


Chelsea FC (@ChelseaFC)

Club statement.

2071605531354210457.headline: Enzo Maresca
2071605531354210457.body: 🗣️ Manchester City statement: Enzo Maresca has long been regarded by the club as a candidate within our succession planning. As a former successful coach within our system, Enzo has a strong relationship with the Club’s leadership and he understands how we work and evolve. We can confirm that confidential conversations took place between select representatives of City and Enzo Maresca in Autumn and Winter of 2025, while he was Chelsea FC’s Head Coach, regarding the possibility of him returning to City in a transitional leadership coaching capacity should Pep Guardiola decide to leave. We acknowledge Chelsea’s statement regarding the context of Enzo’s departure from Chelsea. We also acknowledge the context in which permission for discussions was given, and we recognize that his departure mid-season was disruptive to Chelsea’s season. Once terms were agreed with Enzo Maresca, Manchester City and Chelsea FC reached a mutually agreed settlement under which Chelsea FC will receive compensation. Enzo said: “At the end of December 2025, I made the difficult decision to leave Chelsea. The decision was only mine. My resignation from Chelsea opened a path for me to join Manchester City, which is a club I knew very well. I am ecstatic that I have now joined Manchester City. I recognise that my departure from Chelsea in the middle of the season caused disruption for the club and I apologise for that. It was neither my intention nor my wish. I was treated well by everyone at Chelsea and together we achieved great success and memories that I will always treasure. I am grateful to the club, the owners and the fans for giving me the opportunity. Ben Jacobs (JacobsBen) 🚨 Chelsea have received £17m in compensation following Enzo Maresca's Manchester City appointment. And Maresca has already agreed a separate settlement Maresca has publicly apologised following his Chelsea's resignation, while Chelsea say they feel let down by the Italian's departure since his head and heart were focused on another club. 🗣️ Maresca: The decision was only mine. My resignation from Chelsea opened a path for me to join Manchester City, which is a club I knew very well. I am ecstatic that I have now joined Manchester City. I recognise that my departure from Chelsea in the middle of the season caused disruption for the club and I apologise for that. It was neither my intention nor my wish. Maresca was given permission to speak to Manchester City last year, but at the time Chelsea felt it would be with a view to continuing until the end of the season at the earliest. 🗣️ Chelsea statement: Chelsea FC appreciates the 2025/26 season was a hugely disappointing one for the Club and its supporters. A major contributing factor was the disruption caused as a result of changes that the Club was forced to make to the Head Coach position over the Christmas period. Due to recent developments, we consider that it is important to explain to our supporters what happened and why our former Head Coach left the Club on 1 January 2026. In Autumn last year, the Club was informed by our former Head Coach that there might be an opportunity for him to succeed Pep Guardiola at the end of the season. It became clear to us that it was his strong desire to succeed Guardiola and that he was fully committed to pursuing the opportunity, despite the fact he was under a long-term contract which he had no right to terminate. In December 2025, our Head Coach unexpectedly and abruptly resigned from his position. Obviously, we felt let down as we believed that his head and heart were focused on another club and another opportunity, despite having just arrived at Chelsea the year before. No club wants to change its head coach midway through a season. However, in light of his decision not to continue fulfilling his responsibilities through to the end of the season, the Club was left with no choice but to protect our players, our supporters, and the Badge and accept his resignation. In the circumstances and given the mutual respect between clubs, a confidential settlement has been reached with Manchester City, which includes the payment of compensation. A confidential settlement has also been reached with the former Head Coach under which he will pay compensation. Looking forward to next season, in Xabi Alonso, we have a Manager who has an exceptional football mind and is a professional of the highest integrity. He has all the attributes to deliver the success the Club’s supporters deserve and expect. —
2071605531354210457.raw_text:
🗣️ Manchester City statement: "Enzo Maresca has long been regarded by the club as a candidate within our succession planning. As a former successful coach within our system, Enzo has a strong relationship with the Club’s leadership and he understands how we work and evolve.

"We can confirm that confidential conversations took place between select representatives of City and Enzo Maresca in Autumn and Winter of 2025, while he was Chelsea FC’s Head Coach, regarding the possibility of him returning to City in a transitional leadership coaching capacity should Pep Guardiola decide to leave.

"We acknowledge Chelsea’s statement regarding the context of Enzo’s departure from Chelsea. We also acknowledge the context in which permission for discussions was given, and we recognize that his departure mid-season was disruptive to Chelsea’s season.

"Once terms were agreed with Enzo Maresca, Manchester City and Chelsea FC reached a mutually agreed settlement under which Chelsea FC will receive compensation.

Enzo said: “At the end of December 2025, I made the difficult decision to leave Chelsea.
 
"The decision was only mine. My resignation from Chelsea opened a path for me to join Manchester City, which is a club I knew very well. I am ecstatic that I have now joined Manchester City.
 
"I recognise that my departure from Chelsea in the middle of the season caused disruption for the club and I apologise for that. It was neither my intention nor my wish.
 
"I was treated well by everyone at Chelsea and together we achieved great success and memories that I will always treasure.
 
"I am grateful to the club, the owners and the fans for giving me the opportunity."


Ben Jacobs (@JacobsBen)

🚨 Chelsea have received £17m in compensation following Enzo Maresca's Manchester City appointment. And Maresca has already agreed a separate settlement

Maresca has publicly apologised following his Chelsea's resignation, while Chelsea say they feel "let down" by the Italian's departure since his "head and heart were focused on another club".

🗣️ Maresca: "The decision was only mine. My resignation from Chelsea opened a path for me to join Manchester City, which is a club I knew very well. I am ecstatic that I have now joined Manchester City.   

"I recognise that my departure from Chelsea in the middle of the season caused disruption for the club and I apologise for that. It was neither my intention nor my wish."

Maresca was given permission to speak to Manchester City last year, but at the time Chelsea felt it would be with a view to continuing until the end of the season at the earliest.

🗣️ Chelsea statement: "Chelsea FC appreciates the 2025/26 season was a hugely disappointing one for the Club and its supporters. A major contributing factor was the disruption caused as a result of changes that the Club was forced to make to the Head Coach position over the Christmas period. Due to recent developments, we consider that it is important to explain to our supporters what happened and why our former Head Coach left the Club on 1 January 2026.

"In Autumn last year, the Club was informed by our former Head Coach that there might be an opportunity for him to succeed Pep Guardiola at the end of the season. It became clear to us that it was his strong desire to succeed Guardiola and that he was fully committed to pursuing the opportunity, despite the fact he was under a long-term contract which he had no right to terminate.

"In December 2025, our Head Coach unexpectedly and abruptly resigned from his position. Obviously, we felt let down as we believed that his head and heart were focused on another club and another opportunity, despite having just arrived at Chelsea the year before.

"No club wants to change its head coach midway through a season. However, in light of his decision not to continue fulfilling his responsibilities through to the end of the season, the Club was left with no choice but to protect our players, our supporters, and the Badge and accept his resignation.

"In the circumstances and given the mutual respect between clubs, a confidential settlement has been reached with Manchester City, which includes the payment of compensation. A confidential settlement has also been reached with the former Head Coach under which he will pay compensation.

"Looking forward to next season, in Xabi Alonso, we have a Manager who has an exceptional football mind and is a professional of the highest integrity. He has all the attributes to deliver the success the Club’s supporters deserve and expect."





— https://nitter.net/JacobsBen/status/2071584720698396834#m

2071666550919385127.body: 🚨🆕 Paris Saint-Germain are preparing their opening offer for Yan Diomande. RB Leipzig’s position remains unchanged. Boss Marcel Schäfer told BILD: “Our clear intention is that Yan will play for RB Leipzig next year. And we won’t move away from that! We know exactly what we have in him. So, to make it clear once again: Diomande will remain an RB Leipzig player.” 🇨🇮
2071666550919385127.raw_text:
🚨🆕 Paris Saint-Germain are preparing their opening offer for Yan Diomande. RB Leipzig’s position remains unchanged. Boss Marcel Schäfer told BILD:

“Our clear intention is that Yan will play for RB Leipzig next year. And we won’t move away from that! We know exactly what we have in him. Of course, if Yan continues like this, the time will come when we allow him to take the next step - but not this year.”

“We hold all the cards. So, to make it clear once again: Diomande will remain an RB Leipzig player.” 🇨🇮

2071778245809475812.headline: World Cup
2071778245809475812.body: 🗣️ Julian Nagelsmann says he won't resign following Germany's World Cup exit. I am not someone who runs away. This is not the first time this has happened, and there are some things about today that need to be changed. But if the DFB wants me to continue I am going to continue. I know the mechanics of football. I know how the industry works. I know a lot of people will want me to leave, but I would love to continue if the DFB wants me to.
2071778245809475812.raw_text:
🗣️ Julian Nagelsmann says he won't resign following Germany's World Cup exit. "I am not someone who runs away. This is not the first time this has happened, and there are some things about today that need to be changed. But if the DFB wants me to continue I am going to continue. 

"I know the mechanics of football. I know how the industry works. I know a lot of people will want me to leave, but I would love to continue if the DFB wants me to."

2071879458324099090.headline: Real Madrid

2071912153292951658.headline: World Cup
2071912153292951658.body: Nearly 1,400 illegal World Cup streaming sites have had their advertising revenue cut off as part of the wider fight against digital piracy. The move to deprive the pirates of advertising dollars has been led by the Trustworthy Accountability Group (TAG), a non-profit created by the global advertising industry to fight ad-related crime. And it comes less than a week after the U.S. Department of Justice seized nearly 400 sites that were streaming pirated World Cup content as part of its “Operation Offsides” campaign. More from mjshrimper ⬇️ 🔗 nyti.ms/4v80y8K
2071912153292951658.raw_text:
Nearly 1,400 illegal World Cup streaming sites have had their advertising revenue cut off as part of the wider fight against digital piracy.

The move to deprive the pirates of advertising dollars has been led by the Trustworthy Accountability Group (TAG), a non-profit created by the global advertising industry to fight ad-related crime.

And it comes less than a week after the U.S. Department of Justice seized nearly 400 sites that were streaming pirated World Cup content as part of its “Operation Offsides” campaign.

More from @mjshrimper ⬇️

2071892750492909727.headline: Granit Xhaka
2071892750492909727.body: Chelsea have not given up hope on acquiring Granit Xhaka despite Sunderland’s stance. The 33-year-old, who is keen on the idea of a move to west London, played under new manager Alonso for two years at Bayer Leverkusen. They are determined to sign at least one centre back and Crystal Palace defender Maxence Lacroix is one of their main targets, although no bid has been made. Como’s Jacobo Ramon is also on the wishlist. Chelsea are still keen on Aston Villa’s Morgan Rogers to fill their need for a versatile attacker. They face competition for his signature from Arsenal and Paris Saint-Germain. It will take a large sum to convince Aston Villa to sell. 📝 SJohnsonSport 🔗 nyti.ms/444wZtu
2071892750492909727.raw_text:
Chelsea have not given up hope on acquiring Granit Xhaka despite Sunderland’s stance.

The 33-year-old, who is keen on the idea of a move to west London, played under new manager Alonso for two years at Bayer Leverkusen.

They are determined to sign at least one centre back and Crystal Palace defender Maxence Lacroix is one of their main targets, although no bid has been made. Como’s Jacobo Ramon is also on the wishlist.

Chelsea are still keen on Aston Villa’s Morgan Rogers to fill their need for a versatile attacker. They face competition for his signature from Arsenal and Paris Saint-Germain. It will take a large sum to convince Aston Villa to sell.

2071881678193070182.headline: Real Madrid
2071881678193070182.body: Jose Mourinho still wants another centre-back and another midfielder. Real Madrid are open to this but insist players must leave first. According to sources at Madrid, Borussia Dortmund’s Nico Schlotterbeck is out of the running and Manchester City do not want to sell Ruben Dias. Inter’s Alessandro Bastoni is a player they like. As for midfield, there is no standout name. Enzo Fernandez is Mourinho’s favourite, but club sources have played down the likelihood of his signing. Chelsea are asking for around £120million for him. There have been internal reports about Morocco international Ayyoub Bouaddi since before his breakthrough at the 2026 World Cup, but the 18-year-old is not a priority. Mourinho is also keen on the idea of signing a No 9 in the mould of Joselu, but it is not a priority and for that to happen someone would have to leave the attack, which does not seem likely to happen. 🔗 nyti.ms/444wZtu
2071881678193070182.raw_text:
Jose Mourinho still wants another centre-back and another midfielder. Real Madrid are open to this but insist players must leave first.

According to sources at Madrid, Borussia Dortmund’s Nico Schlotterbeck is out of the running and Manchester City do not want to sell Ruben Dias. Inter’s Alessandro Bastoni is a player they like.

As for midfield, there is no standout name. Enzo Fernandez is Mourinho’s favourite, but club sources have played down the likelihood of his signing. Chelsea are asking for around £120million for him.

There have been internal reports about Morocco international Ayyoub Bouaddi since before his breakthrough at the 2026 World Cup, but the 18-year-old is not a priority.

Mourinho is also keen on the idea of signing a No 9 in the mould of Joselu, but it is not a priority and for that to happen someone would have to leave the attack, which does not seem likely to happen.

🔗 nyti.ms/444wZtu

2071995180924735641.headline: Mateus Fernandes
2071995180924735641.body: Tottenham Hotspur have won the race for West Ham United for midfielder Mateus Fernandes. Spurs have made the highest offer to West Ham — believed to be worth a £85million (€98m, $112m) guaranteed fee according to sources — and the 21-year-old has decided to join them. The Portugal international has attracted interest from multiple clubs, including Manchester United, but is now set to move to north London after West Ham stood firm on their valuation. For United, the transfer fee and the player’s salary went higher than they were willing to pay, and they will now shift their focus to other targets. More from David_Ornstein 🔗 nyti.ms/4gLVR0z
2071995180924735641.raw_text:
Tottenham Hotspur have won the race for West Ham United for midfielder Mateus Fernandes.

Spurs have made the highest offer to West Ham — believed to be worth a £85million (€98m, $112m) guaranteed fee according to sources — and the 21-year-old has decided to join them.

The Portugal international has attracted interest from multiple clubs, including Manchester United, but is now set to move to north London after West Ham stood firm on their valuation.

For United, the transfer fee and the player’s salary went higher than they were willing to pay, and they will now shift their focus to other targets.

More from @David_Ornstein 

🔗 nyti.ms/4gLVR0z

2071994923969032284.headline: Gustavo Alfaro
2071994923969032284.body: Paraguay manager Gustavo Alfaro has become a spiritual leader for his team and he appears to love a press conference. The 63-year-old Argentine is a natural orator and after guiding his team to victory over Germany in the round of 32, his media duties extended beyond an hour. JordanC1107 listened intently to another soliloquy as Alfaro had to be informed he was soon due on a plane to Philadelphia to prepare for their last 16 meeting with France or Sweden. Underestimate them at your peril. 🔗 nyti.ms/4oV1ziX

2071982812056494299.headline: Ansu Fati
2071982812056494299.body: AS Monaco have completed the permanent signing of Ansu Fati on a four-year deal. This is not how fans imagined Fati’s Barcelona career ending, especially back in 2021. Long-term injuries, erratic recovery processes, and an unfair amount of expectation — especially when he was asked to take on the number 10 shirt after Lionel Messi left Barcelona — have hindered Fati’s progress. This move benefits Barcelona’s finances. Besides the €11m profit, his departure will reduce the club’s salaries. 📝 polballus 🔗 nyti.ms/4eBjK9Q
2071982812056494299.raw_text:
AS Monaco have completed the permanent signing of Ansu Fati on a four-year deal.

This is not how fans imagined Fati’s Barcelona career ending, especially back in 2021.

Long-term injuries, erratic recovery processes, and an unfair amount of expectation — especially when he was asked to take on the number 10 shirt after Lionel Messi left Barcelona — have hindered Fati’s progress.

This move benefits Barcelona’s finances. Besides the €11m profit, his departure will reduce the club’s salaries. 

📝 @polballus

2072002674640187892.headline: Squad Cost Ratio
2072002674640187892.body: 🚨 Chelsea fined €3m for a breach of UEFA’s Squad Cost Ratio. €2m of the fine is conditional. 🗣️ “The CFCB First Chamber took into consideration the improving trend in their squad cost ratio between 2024 and 2025 in line with projections submitted as part of their settlement agreement. As a result, part of the fine is conditional upon the clubs continuing to significantly decrease their squad cost ratio in 2026.”

2072000812297998555.headline: Financial Sustainability Regulations
2072000812297998555.body: 🚨 Newcastle United has entered into a settlement agreement with UEFA following a breach of Financial Sustainability Regulations in the three-year period ending June 2025. €3m penalty agreed with a further €7m suspended fine. In addition, UEFA has fined Newcastle a further €3m due to breaching the 70% Squad Cost Ratio (SCR) target in calendar year 2025. 🗣️ “Following an overspend in relation to UEFA's Football Earnings threshold, the club has worked closely and constructively with the Club Financial Control Body (CFCB) to swiftly resolve the matter.”
2072000812297998555.raw_text:
🚨 Newcastle United has entered into a settlement agreement with UEFA following a breach of Financial Sustainability Regulations in the three-year period ending June 2025. €3m penalty agreed with a further €7m suspended fine.

In addition, UEFA has fined Newcastle a further €3m due to breaching the 70% Squad Cost Ratio (SCR) target in calendar year 2025.

🗣️ “Following an overspend in relation to UEFA's Football Earnings threshold, the club has worked closely and constructively with the Club Financial Control Body (CFCB) to swiftly resolve the matter.”

2072002401637167306.headline: World Cup
2072002401637167306.body: This isn’t how I imagined ending the season. Not with West Ham. Not with the Czech Republic. And certainly not with an injury. I’m sorry we couldn’t give our fans the moments and results you deserved. That hurts me more than anything. The good thing about football is that there’s always another challenge waiting. A new season is almost here, bringing a new opportunity to put things right. For 12 years as a professional, I’ve been lucky enough to avoid a serious injury. Now I have to face a challenge I haven’t faced before. But everyone who knows me knows one thing. I never give up! This injury doesn’t change my mindset. If anything, it makes me even more determined. I’ll use these next few weeks/months to work, recover and come back stronger. As a player, as a teammate and as a person. Since the season ended, I’ve only had one thing on my mind. West Ham United. This club means so much to me and I’m ready to do everything I can in helping get it back to where it belongs; to the English Premier League. For the immediate future, I’ll support the boys off the pitch whilst I rehabilitate. Then once back, I’ll give everything I have on the pitch, fighting alongside the boys once again. COYI! As for the international team, I need some time to think about my future. I’m deeply disappointed with how our World Cup campaign ended. I know we are capable of much more in every aspect than we showed, and that’s one of the biggest reasons why this disappointment is so difficult to accept. Representing the Czech Republic has always been the greatest honour of my career. That’s why I don’t want to make any decision in the heat of the moment. I’ll take the time to reflect and decide what’s best for everyone. One thing is certain. I’ll be back. Stronger. Hungrier. More determined than ever. Thank you for standing by me through everything. Your support means more than words can say. ❤️
2072002401637167306.raw_text:
This isn’t how I imagined ending the season.

Not with West Ham. Not with the Czech Republic. And certainly not with an injury.
I’m sorry we couldn’t give our fans the moments and results you deserved. That hurts me more than anything.

The good thing about football is that there’s always another challenge waiting. A new season is almost here, bringing a new opportunity to put things right.

For 12 years as a professional, I’ve been lucky enough to avoid a serious injury. Now I have to face a challenge I haven’t faced before. But everyone who knows me knows one thing.
I never give up!
This injury doesn’t change my mindset. If anything, it makes me even more determined. I’ll use these next few weeks/months to work, recover and come back stronger. As a player, as a teammate and as a person.

Since the season ended, I’ve only had one thing on my mind. West Ham United. This club means so much to me and I’m ready to do everything I can in helping get it back to where it belongs; to the English Premier League.
For the immediate future, I’ll support the boys off the pitch whilst I rehabilitate. Then once back, I’ll give everything I have on the pitch, fighting alongside the boys once again. 
COYI!

As for the international team, I need some time to think about my future. 
I’m deeply disappointed with how our World Cup campaign ended. I know we are capable of much more in every aspect than we showed, and that’s one of the biggest reasons why this disappointment is so difficult to accept.
Representing the Czech Republic has always been the greatest honour of my career. That’s why I don’t want to make any decision in the heat of the moment. I’ll take the time to reflect and decide what’s best for everyone.

One thing is certain.

I’ll be back.

Stronger. Hungrier. More determined than ever.

Thank you for standing by me through everything. Your support means more than words can say. ❤️

2072038850747105387.headline: Transfer update

One who scored and celebrated exactly the way you should celebrate. I am ashamed, as a German, of all the *** who are now defaming and insulting him. Absolutely disgraceful. 2072170901714465132.headline: Ecuador Briefing
2072170901714465132.body: 𝗗𝗶𝗱𝗻’𝘁 𝗙𝗜𝗙𝗔 𝘀𝗮𝘆 𝗶𝘁 𝗵𝗮𝗱 𝗰𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲𝗹𝗹𝗲𝗱 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗵𝘆𝗱𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗯𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗸𝘀? In announcing the delay at the Azteca, FIFA said that as a result the controversial cooling breaks would not happen during Mexico vs Ecuador. But the referee stopped play with around 23 minutes gone. Players walked to their sidelines in customary fashion, and the partisan Mexican crowd jeered briefly before singing “Mexico lindo” to pass the time. On FIFA’s website, the line that “No hydration breaks will take place during this match” had been removed from its statement. Mexico 2-0 Ecuador Briefing: bit.ly/4wECxHB Link Mexico defeat Ecuador for first World Cup knockout win since 1986, how far can Azteca magic take... In announcing the delay at the Azteca, FIFA said that as a result the controversial cooling breaks would not happen during Mexico vs Ecuador.

But the referee stopped play with around 23 minutes gone. Players walked to their sidelines in customary fashion, and the partisan Mexican crowd jeered briefly before singing “Mexico lindo” to pass the time.

On FIFA’s website, the line that “No hydration breaks will take place during this match” had been removed from its statement.

Mexico 2-0 Ecuador Briefing: bit.ly/4wECxHB

2072169735819595801.headline: World Cup
2072169735819595801.body: Mexico beat Ecuador to win their first men's World Cup knockout stage match since 1986. Goals from Julian Quinones and Raul Jimenez — the two scorers in Mexico's 2-0 victory in their World Cup opener against South Africa — earned their country a place in the last 16, where they will face either England or DR Congo in Mexico City on July 5. From the high of their win over Germany, Ecuador, who finished the game with 10 men following Piero Hincapie's red card for covering his mouth in a situation of confrontation, come back down to earth at a raucous Estadio Azteca.
2072169735819595801.raw_text:
Mexico beat Ecuador to win their first men's World Cup knockout stage match since 1986.

Goals from Julian Quinones and Raul Jimenez — the two scorers in Mexico's 2-0 victory in their World Cup opener against South Africa — earned their country a place in the last 16, where they will face either England or DR Congo in Mexico City on July 5.

From the high of their win over Germany, Ecuador, who finished the game with 10 men following Piero Hincapie's red card for covering his mouth in a situation of confrontation, come back down to earth at a raucous Estadio Azteca.

2072238416251519019.headline: Sebastian Beccacece
2072238416251519019.body: Sebastian Beccacece announced his intention to step down as Ecuador head coach after his side exited the World Cup on Tuesday with defeat to Mexico. Ecuador were eliminated at the last-32 stage following a 2-0 loss to the co-hosts in Mexico City. Beccacece told reporters after Wednesday’s defeat that he would not be continuing in the role, explaining that although his side did not achieve what he had hoped, he was proud of “what we built”. More from AliRampling 🔗 nyti.ms/4b0mQlo
2072238416251519019.raw_text:
Sebastian Beccacece announced his intention to step down as Ecuador head coach after his side exited the World Cup on Tuesday with defeat to Mexico.

Ecuador were eliminated at the last-32 stage following a 2-0 loss to the co-hosts in Mexico City.

Beccacece told reporters after Wednesday’s defeat that he would not be continuing in the role, explaining that although his side did not achieve what he had hoped, he was proud of “what we built”.

More from @AliRampling 

🔗 nyti.ms/4b0mQlo

2072281940254658644.headline: Wayne Rooney
2072281940254658644.body: It's been 20 years since Wayne Rooney was shown a red card for stamping on Ricardo Carvalho. Of all the places to stamp as well... Rooney, who had suffered a metatarsal injury less than six weeks before the start of the 2006 World Cup, also shoved then Manchester United teammate Cristiano Ronaldo, who urged Argentine referee Horacio Elizondo to brandish a red card. Ronaldo later scored the winning spot kick as Portugal eventually won 3-1 on penalties: Frank Lampard, Steven Gerrard and Jamie Carragher failed to convert for England.
2072281940254658644.raw_text:
It's been 20 years since Wayne Rooney was shown a red card for stamping on Ricardo Carvalho.

Of all the places to stamp as well...

Rooney, who had suffered a metatarsal injury less than six weeks before the start of the 2006 World Cup, also shoved then Manchester United teammate Cristiano Ronaldo, who urged Argentine referee Horacio Elizondo to brandish a red card.

Ronaldo later scored the winning spot kick as Portugal eventually won 3-1 on penalties: Frank Lampard, Steven Gerrard and Jamie Carragher failed to convert for England.

2072307349847491051.headline: World Cup
2072307349847491051.body: Three people have died from suffocation during World Cup celebrations in Mexico. A 19-year-old woman, a 44-year-old man and a 48-year-old woman died after crowds took to the streets in Mexico City, the nation’s capital, following the country’s round-of-32 victory over Ecuador on Tuesday, according to the city’s public health authority. The public health authority added that medical responders had performed CPR on all three of the victims but each had died from asphyxiation and been identified by their families. ✍️ AliRampling 🔗 nyti.ms/4ggOiyU
2072307349847491051.raw_text:
Three people have died from suffocation during World Cup celebrations in Mexico.

A 19-year-old woman, a 44-year-old man and a 48-year-old woman died after crowds took to the streets in Mexico City, the nation’s capital, following the country’s round-of-32 victory over Ecuador on Tuesday, according to the city’s public health authority.

The public health authority added that medical responders had performed CPR on all three of the victims but each had died from asphyxiation and been identified by their families.

✍️ @AliRampling 

🔗 nyti.ms/4ggOiyU

2072309060884410382.headline: World Cup
2072309060884410382.body: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has also fallen foul of Germany’s World Cup elimination by Paraguay, after stumbling into a social media debacle following a communications blunder. In the minutes after the Germans were eliminated on penalties for the first time in their World Cup history, a post from Merz’s official X account was published, reading: “Even if elimination hurts: what a game. With your commitment and team spirit at this World Cup,” the post continued, addressing the beaten players, “you have thrilled our country. We are proud of you.” Germans were not thrilled by their team’s World Cup performances. They were certainly not proud of them, either, and were quick to respond to Merz’s post. Among the most popular responses were users accusing Chancellor Merz of being “detached” or, as is common among European leaders, using football to communicate with voters. According to the Chancellor’s office, Merz’s post was a due to a communications error resulting in the incorrect social media post had been sent. ✍️ SebSB 🔗 nyti.ms/3QUplih
2072309060884410382.raw_text:
German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has also fallen foul of Germany’s World Cup elimination by Paraguay, after stumbling into a social media debacle following a communications blunder.

In the minutes after the Germans were eliminated on penalties for the first time in their World Cup history, a post from Merz’s official X account was published, reading: “Even if elimination hurts: what a game. With your commitment and team spirit at this World Cup,” the post continued, addressing the beaten players, “you have thrilled our country. We are proud of you.”

Germans were not thrilled by their team’s World Cup performances. They were certainly not proud of them, either, and were quick to respond to Merz’s post. Among the most popular responses were users accusing Chancellor Merz of being “detached” or, as is common among European leaders, using football to communicate with voters.

According to the Chancellor’s office, Merz’s post was a due to a communications error resulting in the incorrect social media post had been sent.

✍️ @SebSB 

🔗 nyti.ms/3QUplih

2072313276386467944.headline: Joan Laporta
2072313276386467944.body: 🚨 Joan Laporta: “We have a lot of respect for Atlético Madrid.. The tweets were before Julián spoke about his dream of playing for a big club, but he never mentioned Barcelona at any point; we did NOT force him into this”. “The player was on our radar even before he moved to Manchester City. What we did was a club-to-club offer. I see NO logic in the actions they're taking now”. “This isn't the first time we've done a deal with Atlético, but perhaps this one is more sensitive; we're putting in a great effort, and there are those who want to stir up controversy”.
2072313276386467944.raw_text:
🚨 Joan Laporta: “We have a lot of respect for Atlético Madrid.. The tweets were before Julián spoke about his dream of playing for a big club, but he never mentioned Barcelona at any point; we did NOT force him into this”.

“The player was on our radar even before he moved to Manchester City. What we did was a club-to-club offer. I see NO logic in the actions they're taking now”.

“This isn't the first time we've done a deal with Atlético, but perhaps this one is more sensitive; we're putting in a great effort, and there are those who want to stir up controversy”.

2072312938866655441.headline: Julián Álvarez
2072312938866655441.body: 🚨🔵🔴 Laporta on Julián Álvarez: “We will keep this offer firm for as long as we consider. But we will not be at the expense of what Atlético de Madrid can tell us on this issue”. “They know what our will is, if they want to do it, we will be delighted”. “At the moment, Atlético says they do NOT sell Álvarez. Now, they don’t sell him because seems they had no alternative...”. “I hope that if Atlético Madrid can find an alternative, they will consider our bid”.
2072312938866655441.raw_text:
🚨🔵🔴 Laporta on Julián Álvarez: “We will keep this offer firm for as long as we consider. But we will not be at the expense of what Atlético de Madrid can tell us on this issue”.

“They know what our will is, if they want to do it, we will be delighted”.

“At the moment, Atlético says they do NOT sell Álvarez. Now, they don’t sell him because seems they had no alternative...”.

“I hope that if Atlético Madrid can find an alternative, they will consider our bid”.

2072312623761170914.headline: Miguel Angel Gil
2072312623761170914.body: 🚨 Barça president Laporta: “We made this offer for Julián with all respect to Atlético. I spoke to both Cerezo and Miguel Angel Gil”. “The offer was sent and Atlético told me they had NO plan to sell Álvarez because they had NO alternatige as striker”, he added. “I told them that, if they have alternatives, the bid is firm”.

2072318314550903147.headline: Marcus Rashford
2072318314550903147.body: Marcus Rashford will keep his place when England take on DR Congo in the World Cup round of 32 later today. The Manchester United forward started and impressed in the final group-stage game against Panama and remains in the starting XI chosen by Thomas Tuchel, preferred over Anthony Gordon. Noni Madueke also starts with Bukayo Saka continuing to manage his fitness. Arsenal team-mate Declan Rice returns to the side after sitting out Saturday's match. Morgan Rogers started that game but drops back down to the bench in Atlanta. Djed Spence will start at right-back with Jarell Quansah and Reece James already having been ruled out with injury. ✍️ David_Ornstein 🔗 nyti.ms/4wj0iEH
2072318314550903147.raw_text:
Marcus Rashford will keep his place when England take on DR Congo in the World Cup round of 32 later today.

The Manchester United forward started and impressed in the final group-stage game against Panama and remains in the starting XI chosen by Thomas Tuchel, preferred over Anthony Gordon.

Noni Madueke also starts with Bukayo Saka continuing to manage his fitness. Arsenal team-mate Declan Rice returns to the side after sitting out Saturday's match.

Morgan Rogers started that game but drops back down to the bench in Atlanta.

Djed Spence will start at right-back with Jarell Quansah and Reece James already having been ruled out with injury.

✍️ @David_Ornstein 

🔗 nyti.ms/4wj0iEH

2072327238863782374.headline: Cristiano Ronaldo
2072327238863782374.body: Cristiano Ronaldo is yet to miss a minute of action at the World Cup and the notion of dropping one of the all-time greats is unthinkable, and - irrespective of the fact he intends to leave his role at the end of the tournament - unlikely to be a battle Roberto Martinez wants to take on. But, other than when Portugal are in the attacking third, they are playing with nine outfield players. Ronaldo does not track back, nor is he going to start doing that at this stage of his career. So, the onus is on those around him to do the running instead. And this is something of which Nani has first-hand experience at a major tournament having been asked to do it ahead of their Euro 2016 triumph by Fernando Santos, then Portugal’s head coach. “We need to understand that Brazil will do it (run) for Neymar,” he adds. “France will do for (Kylian) Mbappe, Argentina will do it for (Lionel) Messi. There is no discussion in these teams because and don’t have people so famous like Ronaldo. “Messi is always walking because he saves energy when the ball comes to him. He’s explosive and at his best in the the final third. “Ronaldo is the same in that it’s almost like: ‘Don’t waste your energy sprinting 60 yards.’” Read Nani’s exclusive interview with Dan_Sheldon_ ⤵️ 🔗 nyti.ms/4v9DdDx
2072327238863782374.raw_text:
Cristiano Ronaldo is yet to miss a minute of action at the World Cup and the notion of dropping one of the all-time greats is unthinkable, and - irrespective of the fact he intends to leave his role at the end of the tournament - unlikely to be a battle Roberto Martinez wants to take on.

But, other than when Portugal are in the attacking third, they are playing with nine outfield players. Ronaldo does not track back, nor is he going to start doing that at this stage of his career.

So, the onus is on those around him to do the running instead. And this is something of which Nani has first-hand experience at a major tournament having been asked to do it ahead of their Euro 2016 triumph by Fernando Santos, then Portugal’s head coach.

“We need to understand that Brazil will do it (run) for Neymar,” he adds. “France will do for (Kylian) Mbappe, Argentina will do it for (Lionel) Messi. There is no discussion in these teams because and don’t have people so famous like Ronaldo.

“Messi is always walking because he saves energy when the ball comes to him. He’s explosive and at his best in the the final third.

“Ronaldo is the same in that it’s almost like: ‘Don’t waste your energy sprinting 60 yards.’”

Read Nani’s exclusive interview with @Dan_Sheldon_ ⤵️

🔗 nyti.ms/4v9DdDx

2072333404289327325.headline: Declan Rice
2072333404289327325.body: Thomas Tuchel’s tweaks to England's line-up against DR Congo likely reflect a couple of things: 🔎 Declan Rice’s inclusion ahead of Morgan Rogers suggests he wants more protection against the counter-attacking threat of DR Congo, who will look to break from their 5-3-2 block 🔎 Djed Spence’s recovery pace at right-back should help, too, as Tuchel moves away from the six-attacker team he picked to try to break down Panama 🔎 Expect England to attack with five players across the front line, with left-back Nico O’Reilly stepping into midfield. 🔎 Marcus Rashford being preferred to Anthony Gordon on the left is partly because he has been the more effective of the two at this tournament, and partly because his inswinging crosses caused problems against Panama 📝 LiamTharmeCoach 🔗 nyti.ms/4vQKhpJ
2072333404289327325.raw_text:
Thomas Tuchel’s tweaks to England's line-up against DR Congo likely reflect a couple of things:

🔎 Declan Rice’s inclusion ahead of Morgan Rogers suggests he wants more protection against the counter-attacking threat of DR Congo, who will look to break from their 5-3-2 block

🔎 Djed Spence’s recovery pace at right-back should help, too, as Tuchel moves away from the six-attacker team he picked to try to break down Panama

🔎 Expect England to attack with five players across the front line, with left-back Nico O’Reilly stepping into midfield.

🔎 Marcus Rashford being preferred to Anthony Gordon on the left is partly because he has been the more effective of the two at this tournament, and partly because his inswinging crosses caused problems against Panama

2072366578083979359.headline: Vitor Pereira
2072366578083979359.body: 🚨 BREAKING: Vitor Pereira statement confirming his Nottingham Forest departure. Today marks the end of my journey as Head Coach of Nottingham Forest. I want to say a sincere thank you to everyone connected with this incredible football club. Although this decision came as a complete surprise to me and without any warning, I fully respect the club's right to make the decisions it believes are best for its future. Naturally, I am disappointed and saddened. I truly believed in what we were building together, and I leave with a sense of pride in everything we achieved over the past months. Together, we enjoyed a memorable end to the season. We secured the club's Premier League status, reached the semi-finals of the Europa League, and created moments that will stay with me forever. Most importantly, I saw a group of players grow in confidence, belief and togetherness. Watching them develop, fight for one another and represent this club with passion has been one of the most rewarding experiences of my career. I would like to thank my coaching staff, the players, everyone behind the scenes, and especially the supporters. From my first day, your passion, loyalty and unwavering support made me feel incredibly welcome. You were with us through every challenge and every success, and I will always be grateful for that. I leave Nottingham Forest with no bitterness or resentment—only respect, gratitude and wonderful memories. Football is full of unexpected moments, and while this chapter has ended sooner than I expected, I will always look back on my time here with pride and affection. I wish the players, the staff, the supporters and everyone at Nottingham Forest every success in the future. This is a special club, and I sincerely hope it continues to grow and achieve great things.
2072366578083979359.raw_text:
🚨 BREAKING: Vitor Pereira statement confirming his Nottingham Forest departure.

"Today marks the end of my journey as Head Coach of Nottingham Forest.

"I want to say a sincere thank you to everyone connected with this incredible football club. Although this decision came as a complete surprise to me and without any warning, I fully respect the club's right to make the decisions it believes are best for its future.

"Naturally, I am disappointed and saddened. I truly believed in what we were building together, and I leave with a sense of pride in everything we achieved over the past months.

"Together, we enjoyed a memorable end to the season. We secured the club's Premier League status, reached the semi-finals of the Europa League, and created moments that will stay with me forever. Most importantly, I saw a group of players grow in confidence, belief and togetherness. Watching them develop, fight for one another and represent this club with passion has been one of the most rewarding experiences of my career.

"I would like to thank my coaching staff, the players, everyone behind the scenes, and especially the supporters. From my first day, your passion, loyalty and unwavering support made me feel incredibly welcome. You were with us through every challenge and every success, and I will always be grateful for that.

"I leave Nottingham Forest with no bitterness or resentment—only respect, gratitude and wonderful memories. Football is full of unexpected moments, and while this chapter has ended sooner than I expected, I will always look back on my time here with pride and affection.

"I wish the players, the staff, the supporters and everyone at Nottingham Forest every success in the future. This is a special club, and I sincerely hope it continues to grow and achieve great things."

2072368904769609861.headline: Harry Kane
2072368904769609861.body: The verdict on Harry Kane's penalty claim from former Premier League referee Graham Scott (refsplaining): Harry Kane only has himself to blame: by dragging his left foot into the onrushing goalkeeper, he created doubt in the referee’s mind, and his penalty claim was waved away. On first viewing, England fans – including me – were no doubt screaming ‘penalty’ at their TV screens, and many referees would have agreed. There is a good case for one, as goalkeeper Lionel Mpasi undoubtedly makes significant contact with Kane, and there is no way the VAR would have recommended a review had Jordanian referee Adham Makhadmeh pointed to the spot. However, Kane goes to great lengths to ensure that contact occurs, plants both feet on the ground and falls theatrically – all clues to the referee that the England striker was having him on. Fair play to Makhadmeh for standing strong. Had Kane carried on running as normal, he may have been brought down anyway – and earned the penalty he craved – or he might have reached the ball and rolled it into the empty net. I wish he had. England vs DR Congo live blog: bit.ly/4vS1z5G
2072368904769609861.raw_text:
The verdict on Harry Kane's penalty claim from former Premier League referee Graham Scott (@refsplaining):

"Harry Kane only has himself to blame: by dragging his left foot into the onrushing goalkeeper, he created doubt in the referee’s mind, and his penalty claim was waved away.

"On first viewing, England fans – including me – were no doubt screaming ‘penalty’ at their TV screens, and many referees would have agreed.

"There is a good case for one, as goalkeeper Lionel Mpasi undoubtedly makes significant contact with Kane, and there is no way the VAR would have recommended a review had Jordanian referee Adham Makhadmeh pointed to the spot.

"However, Kane goes to great lengths to ensure that contact occurs, plants both feet on the ground and falls theatrically – all clues to the referee that the England striker was having him on. Fair play to Makhadmeh for standing strong.

"Had Kane carried on running as normal, he may have been brought down anyway – and earned the penalty he craved – or he might have reached the ball and rolled it into the empty net. I wish he had."

England vs DR Congo live blog: bit.ly/4vS1z5G

2072388738559139994.headline: Sandro Tonali
2072388738559139994.body: Tottenham Hotspur have reached to an agreement to sign Sandro Tonali from Newcastle United in a deal that could reach £100million. The north London club stepped up their pursuit of the Italy international late last month by initiating club-to-club contact with Newcastle. They have now struck a deal which, if completed, would be a new club-record transfer eclipsing the potential £85m they have already agreed to pay West Ham United for fellow midfielder Mateus Fernandes. More from David_Ornstein 🔗 nyti.ms/4eR2Kv1
2072388738559139994.raw_text:
Tottenham Hotspur have reached to an agreement to sign Sandro Tonali from Newcastle United in a deal that could reach £100million.

The north London club stepped up their pursuit of the Italy international late last month by initiating club-to-club contact with Newcastle.

They have now struck a deal which, if completed, would be a new club-record transfer eclipsing the potential £85m they have already agreed to pay West Ham United for fellow midfielder Mateus Fernandes.

deferred.headline: Atletico Madrid
deferred.body: Atletico Madrid is being assessed and the club is monitoring the situation.
deferred.text:
⏳🚨 Bayer 04 Leverkusen are willing to sell Alejandro #Grimaldo and are already working on a replacement.
Initial asking price is €30m as reported. An agreement from around €25m upwards cannot be ruled out.
Leverkusen do not want to block the move. Grimaldo wants to leave and already has a full agreement in place with Atletico Madrid.
Negotiations between the clubs are ongoing.
 🇪🇸

deferred.headline: Michael Olise
deferred.body: Michael Olise is set to stay; no exit is planned at this stage.
deferred.text:
🚨❌ FC Bayern have also received confirmation from Michael Olise’s camp that there have been no talks, meetings or negotiations with Real Madrid so far.
No offer from Real Madrid either. Bayern are not open to negotiations. Olise has not expressed any desire to leave.
Instead, Bayern are working behind the scenes on contract extension beyond 2029 and want to make him one of the club’s top earners.  🇫🇷

deferred.headline: Jones
deferred.body: Jones is set to stay at Liverpool; no exit is planned at this stage.
deferred.text:
Inter made a fresh approach for Curtis Jones last week asking whether a deal was possible for around €25m.
This was a conversation not a formal or written offer.
Liverpool made it clear no sale at this price, especially given numbers other midfielders are expected to be sold for this summer.
Liverpool are prepared to keep Jones unless a suitable bid arises, even if that means losing him on a free next summer.
Liverpool have never had concerns about Jones' attitude or professionalism despite Inter reapproaching the club, and growing interest elsewhere.
Understand the club's position has hardened as a result of Inter's latest contact. If the right offer emerges, a sale will be sanctioned, but Inter would have to significantly increase their valuation to get Liverpool to engage.
Worth noting, Liverpool are also comfortable with Jones staying, and if that happens, and he impresses Andoni Iraola, they could yet decide to engage in new contract talks.🔴
deferred.draft_caption:
📝 REPORTED: Jones (Liverpool)
Jones is set to stay at Liverpool; no exit is planned at this stage.
